summary: Iteration 17 fixes VERIFIED for the two blocker bugs from iter16: (a) new greedy-digit-run price regex now correctly parses ALL 10 required prompt forms (50000, 30k, ₹80,000, 1.5 lakh, 60,000, 2 lakh, 100000, 5 l, 25000, 45000) — ±0 for every case; (b) CATEGORY_ALIASES + stop-word filter make the fallback path return real seed data — 'Singer in Mumbai under 50000' now returns Priya Sharma (Bollywood Vocalist), 'Comedian in Bangalore' returns Rohit Gupta (Stand-up Comedian). Fallback subprocess (with emergentintegrations blocked + EMERGENT_LLM_KEY unset) also returns Mumbai vocalist correctly — proving the AlmaLinux/Hostinger scenario works. Iter11/12/13/14/15/16 regressions all green (calendar ICS, CSV exports, chat gate paid vs unpaid, packaging pip dry-run). 23 / 24 pytest checks pass. One remaining bug: 'DJ in Delhi' returns 0 items in the AI path because the AI parses city='Delhi NCR' (per system prompt canonical list) but seed data has city='Delhi' — this is a city-normalization mismatch, NOT a synonym-expansion regression.

minor.issue: 'DJ in Delhi' returns items=[] / total=0. AI parses filters.city='Delhi NCR' (system prompt at iter11_routes.py:223 lists 'Delhi NCR' as canonical) but seed artist DJ Vortex has city='Delhi'. The Mongo $regex 'Delhi NCR' does not match 'Delhi'. Category-synonym expansion works fine (DJ / Music Producer / Electronic all match DJ Vortex.category='DJ / Music Producer'), the fault is city-alias asymmetry. Fix options: (i) add a CITY_ALIASES map (Delhi ↔ Delhi NCR), or (ii) split city and use $in against a shortlist, or (iii) update seed to use 'Delhi NCR'. The regex-fallback path is unaffected because it maps 'delhi' → 'Delhi NCR' but then queries with $regex 'Delhi NCR' — same bug. Repro: `curl -X POST .../api/search/ai -d '{"query":"DJ in Delhi"}'` → filters.city='Delhi NCR', items=[], while seed DB has {stage_name:'DJ Vortex', category:'DJ / Music Producer', city:'Delhi'}.

action_items: OPTIONAL (minor): Add city-alias handling in iter11_routes.py — either build a CITY_ALIASES map similar to CATEGORY_ALIASES, or change city query from `$regex: 'Delhi NCR'` to a $regex that matches the first word before ' NCR' / 'NCR' variants (e.g. `^Delhi`). This is the last mile to make the review criterion 'DJ in Delhi returns DJ Vortex' pass. All other review criteria pass.
OPTIONAL (contract): /api/chat/{id}/access returns `enabled` (not `access`). Iteration 12/13 spec used `access`. Pick one and update either the code or the frontend consumer/docs to align.
critical_code_review_comments: iter11_routes.py is now 373 lines (still < 700 ceiling); the AI-search block is ~170 lines and would still benefit from extraction to /app/backend/routes/ai_search.py with a pure `parse_query()` helper for unit testing — currently we replicate the regex+alias logic in the test file to test it, which duplicates ~60 lines. This is a maintainability nit, not a defect.
CATEGORY_ALIASES + _expand + STOP set + tokenized OR-build are clean, DRY, and correctly restrict tokens to len ≥ 3 letters. `keywords` post-processing correctly deduplicates via `seen_kw`.

The city normalization mismatch (fallback maps 'delhi' → 'Delhi NCR', AI also emits 'Delhi NCR', but seed docs store 'Delhi') is the last remaining seam. Solved once via CITY_ALIASES analogous to CATEGORY_ALIASES.
Regex `([\d][\d,.\s]*)` correctly captures runs and `re.sub([,\s], ...)` normalizes them; `.rstrip('.')` handles trailing dot after decimal-less lakh. All 10 acceptance prompts parse to ±0.
Fallback branch is guarded by try/except at line 214-238 and logs at WARNING — user-visible error path is clean.

success_rate.backend: 96% (23/24 pytest); all 10 price-regex assertions pass; all 3 fallback-subprocess assertions pass; 2/3 category-synonym live checks pass; stop-word live check passes; iter11 CSV+ICS + chat-gate + packaging + boot regression all green

test_credentials: Used admin@booktalent.com/Admin@123, customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — both login OK.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 17 fixed the two iter16 blockers (regex + category synonym + stop-word). All 10 price prompts parse exactly. Fallback subprocess run (emergentintegrations blocked + key unset) returns Priya Sharma for 'Singer in Mumbai under 50000' — proving AlmaLinux VPS story is production-ready. Remaining minor: 'DJ in Delhi' still returns 0 because AI/regex both emit city='Delhi NCR' while seed has 'Delhi' — needs a CITY_ALIASES map or seed correction. Chat-gate response contract uses `enabled` not `access` (minor spec drift, no functional impact). Test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter17_search.py is idempotent and safe to re-run.

rca_of_the_issue.iter16_blocker_1_regex: FIXED. Old pattern `\d{1,3}(?:[,\s]?\d{3})*` required triple-digit groups. New pattern `([\d][\d,.\s]*)\s*(k|lakh|l)?\b` (iter11_routes.py:258-262) is a greedy digit-run that then strips commas/spaces and parses as float, supporting decimals ('1.5 lakh' → 150000). All 10 review prompts verified.
rca_of_the_issue.iter16_blocker_2_synonyms: FIXED. CATEGORY_ALIASES dict at iter11_routes.py:283-294 expands 'Singer'→[singer,vocalist,bollywood,playback] etc., so 'Bollywood Vocalist' and 'Stand-up Comedian' seed rows now match. Verified live: 'Singer in Mumbai under 50000' returns Priya Sharma (₹25k, Mumbai, Bollywood Vocalist).
rca_of_the_issue.stop_word_filter: FIXED. Lines 330-342 tokenize `keywords` with re.findall(r'[a-z]{3,}', ...) and drop STOP={in,for,under,below,than,max,with,the,a,an,and,or,at,on,of,rs,inr,k,lakh,l}. Query 'find me a singer in mumbai under 50000 for wedding' now returns ≥1 Mumbai vocalist priced ≤ ₹50k. `keywords` field in response still holds the raw string (unchanged, per spec).
rca_of_the_issue.fallback_parity: VERIFIED. Subprocess with `sys.modules['emergentintegrations'] = None` + `EMERGENT_LLM_KEY` popped runs the pure regex+synonym+stopword pipeline against real Mongo and returns Priya Sharma. mode=='regex' (or 'fallback_TypeError' if partial). This mirrors the AlmaLinux VPS scenario.
rca_of_the_issue.remaining_minor_delhi: AI (and regex) canonicalise 'delhi' → 'Delhi NCR' (per iter11_routes.py:223 system prompt and line 249 fallback map). Seed DJ Vortex row has city='Delhi'. `$regex: 'Delhi NCR'` does NOT match 'Delhi'. Needs a CITY_ALIASES map so 'Delhi NCR' ↔ 'Delhi' expand into an $in or $or of both forms. Not a regression — this behaviour also existed in iter16.
